description_long: This guard stone is a robust, geometric structure composed of stacked cylindrical and rectangular stone blocks, with a prominent triangular face featuring a set of small circular holes. The central section consists of three thick, rounded stone rings, topped by a tall, angular slab that leans slightly to one side. Flanking the main body are two large, wedge-shaped stone pieces, one of which forms a pronounced triangular front. The base is a solid rectangular slab, providing stability and mass. The surface is uniformly gray, with a matte, rough texture indicative of weathered stone. The overall form is utilitarian, designed to prevent vehicle encroachment or protect corners in urban environments.
description: A heavy, gray stone guard stone with stacked cylindrical and rectangular forms, featuring a triangular front with circular holes and a tall, angular slab on top. Its rough, matte surface and solid construction suggest durability and urban utility.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the triangular face with circular holes and the stacked cylindrical core, with the tall slab rising behind.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the layered cylindrical rings and the angular slab, with the rectangular base and side wedge visible.
description_view_2: The rear three-quarter view shows the large wedge-shaped side and the back of the triangular face, with the tall slab leaning away.
description_view_3: The back view reveals the stacked cylindrical rings and both side wedges, with the angular slab centered at the top.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban sidewalks, street corners, or at the entrance to driveways and building perimeters to prevent vehicle intrusion or protect infrastructure.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright on a flat surface, with the base flush against pavement or concrete.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on edges and corners from vehicle contact, as well as surface erosion and discoloration from weather exposure. The base may exhibit grime or staining from street runoff.
